about.heading: Registered physiotherapists focused on you
about.body:
Restore Physio has been providing evidence-based physiotherapy to patients on the North Shore for 9 years. Our team of registered physiotherapists hold current AHPRA registration and undertake ongoing professional development to stay current with best-practice guidelines.

We take time to understand your history, work and activity goals before developing a treatment plan. We work with private health funds, icare, DVA and Medicare chronic disease management plans.

service_area.heading: Serving the North Shore and surrounds
service_area.intro: Our Chatswood clinic is well-placed for patients from across the North Shore. If your suburb is not listed, give us a call.

testimonials.quote: The team at Restore Physio are thorough and professional. They explained every step of the assessment and made sure I understood the treatment plan before we started.
testimonials.author: Michael R.

testimonials.quote: Easy to book, on time for appointments, and the reception staff are friendly. The whole clinic runs smoothly.
testimonials.author: Sarah K.

testimonials.quote: My physio took the time to go through my scan results and explain what they meant. I appreciated not being rushed.
testimonials.author: James L.

offer.headline: New patient initial consultation
offer.description: A thorough 60-minute initial assessment covering your history, movement, and a clear treatment plan. No GP referral required.

services.title: Sports Injury Rehabilitation
services.summary: Assessment, treatment and return-to-sport programs for acute and chronic sports injuries across all activity levels.
services.intro:
Sports injuries range from acute muscle strains and ligament sprains to chronic overuse conditions that develop over time. At Restore Physio, our registered physiotherapists conduct a thorough assessment to identify the nature and extent of your injury before developing an individualised treatment plan.

We work with recreational and competitive athletes across the North Shore, tailoring rehabilitation programs to your specific sport, position, training load and return-to-sport goals. Treatment may include manual therapy, exercise prescription, taping and load management strategies.
benefits: Thorough initial assessment covering movement, strength and load tolerance
Treatment plans aligned with your return-to-sport timeline
Exercise programs you can continue between sessions
Injury prevention strategies to support ongoing participation
Liaison with coaches and trainers where appropriate
Private health fund rebates available on the spot

sections.heading: Common conditions we treat
sections.body: Our physiotherapists regularly assess and treat: ankle sprains and ligament injuries, hamstring and quadriceps strains, shoulder rotator cuff injuries, ACL and knee ligament sprains, Achilles tendinopathy, patellofemoral pain, stress fractures and bone stress reactions, shoulder impingement and bursitis, and wrist and hand injuries from racquet sports and contact sports.

sections.heading: Return-to-sport planning
sections.body: A structured return-to-sport plan sets out the criteria you need to meet at each stage before progressing. This helps manage your risk of re-injury and gives you clear targets to work toward. We can collaborate with your club physio, GP or specialist to ensure a coordinated approach.

faqs.question: Do I need a referral to see a physiotherapist for a sports injury?
faqs.answer: No GP referral is required to book an appointment. You can contact us directly. If you are claiming through a Medicare chronic disease management plan or a workers compensation insurer, a referral from your GP will be needed.

faqs.question: Can I claim sports physio on private health insurance?
faqs.answer: Yes, most private health funds with extras cover include physiotherapy. Rebates vary by fund and policy level. We have HICAPS on site so you can claim on the spot and pay only the gap.

faqs.question: How long does a sports injury rehabilitation program take?
faqs.answer: This depends on the type and severity of the injury, your activity goals, and how your body responds to treatment. Some acute injuries resolve in a few weeks; complex ligament or post-surgical rehabilitation may take several months. Your physiotherapist will outline a realistic timeframe at your initial assessment.

seo.title: Sports Injury Rehabilitation | Restore Physio Chatswood
seo.description: Sports injury assessment and rehabilitation in Chatswood, NSW. Registered physiotherapists treating strains, sprains, overuse injuries and return-to-sport programs across the North Shore.

services.title: Back & Neck Pain
services.summary: Evidence-based physiotherapy for acute and chronic back pain, neck stiffness, sciatica and disc-related conditions.
services.intro:
Back and neck pain affects most people at some point, and for some it becomes a persistent problem that interferes with work, sleep and daily activities. Physiotherapy can help you understand what is driving your pain and provide evidence-based strategies to manage it.

Our physiotherapists assess your movement, strength, posture and lifestyle factors before developing a treatment plan. This may include manual therapy, targeted exercise, education about pain and load management, and workplace ergonomics advice.
benefits: Thorough assessment of movement, posture and contributing factors
Manual therapy to improve joint mobility and reduce muscle tension
Targeted exercise programs to build strength and support recovery
Education about your condition in plain language
Workplace ergonomics review for desk-related pain
Coordination with your GP or specialist if imaging is needed

sections.heading: Conditions we commonly assess and treat
sections.body: Our physiotherapists work with patients experiencing: acute lower back pain and lumbar disc conditions, chronic lower back pain, neck pain and stiffness, cervicogenic headaches, sciatica and referred leg pain, thoracic pain and mid-back stiffness, postural pain from desk work, whiplash from motor vehicle accidents, and spondylosis and facet joint conditions.

sections.heading: What to expect at your first appointment
sections.body: Your initial consultation is 60 minutes. We will take a detailed history of your symptoms, conduct a physical assessment and explain what we found. You will leave with a clear understanding of your condition and an agreed treatment plan, including how many sessions are likely to be needed and what you can do between appointments to support your progress.

faqs.question: Should I rest or keep moving when I have back pain?
faqs.answer: For most people, staying as active as comfort allows produces better outcomes than bed rest. Your physiotherapist will advise on appropriate activity levels, safe movements and any activities to avoid in the short term based on your assessment findings.

faqs.question: Do I need an MRI or X-ray before seeing a physiotherapist?
faqs.answer: Not usually. Most back and neck conditions can be assessed clinically. Your physiotherapist will advise if imaging is likely to change the management approach. If imaging is needed, we can liaise with your GP.

seo.title: Back & Neck Pain Physiotherapy | Restore Physio Chatswood
seo.description: Evidence-based physio for back pain, neck pain, sciatica and disc conditions in Chatswood and the North Shore. Registered physiotherapists, same-day appointments often available.

services.title: Post-Surgical Rehabilitation
services.summary: Structured physiotherapy programs following joint replacement, ligament reconstruction and spinal surgery.
services.intro:
Post-surgical rehabilitation is a structured, progressive process that helps you regain movement, strength and function after an operation. The right physiotherapy program, started at the right time, is a key part of achieving a good surgical outcome.

Our physiotherapists work closely with surgeons and specialists across the North Shore to provide rehabilitation that follows evidence-based protocols and aligns with your surgeon's post-operative guidelines.
benefits: Protocols aligned with your surgeon's post-operative guidelines
Progressive rehabilitation from early mobility to full function
Strengthening and neuromuscular control exercises
Scar tissue and swelling management
Clear milestones and progress tracking
Return-to-work and return-to-sport planning

sections.heading: Surgeries we commonly support
sections.body: We provide post-surgical physiotherapy following: total knee replacement, total hip replacement, ACL reconstruction and other knee ligament surgeries, rotator cuff repair, shoulder stabilisation procedures, spinal discectomy and fusion, ankle ligament reconstruction, carpal tunnel release, and elbow and wrist procedures.

sections.heading: Working alongside your surgical team
sections.body: We are happy to contact your surgeon or specialist directly to clarify post-operative instructions and ensure our program aligns with their expectations. If you have a surgical report or discharge summary, please bring it to your first appointment or send it ahead of time.

faqs.question: When should I start physiotherapy after surgery?
faqs.answer: Timing depends on the procedure. Some surgeries benefit from physiotherapy within days; others require a period of protected weight-bearing or rest first. Your surgeon will advise on the appropriate start time, and we can coordinate directly with their rooms if needed.

faqs.question: Can I claim post-surgical physio on Medicare?
faqs.answer: Medicare does not typically rebate standard physiotherapy consultations. However, if your GP has set up a chronic disease management plan, you may be eligible for a limited number of subsidised sessions. Private health insurance extras cover usually applies.

seo.title: Post-Surgical Rehabilitation | Restore Physio Chatswood North Shore
seo.description: Post-surgical physiotherapy in Chatswood, NSW. Structured rehab programs following knee replacement, ACL reconstruction, shoulder surgery and more. Registered physiotherapists.

services.title: Workplace Injury (Workers Comp)
services.summary: Approved provider for icare NSW workers compensation claims. Workplace injury assessment, treatment and return-to-work support.
services.intro:
Workplace injuries can affect your ability to do your job and your quality of life outside work. As an approved icare provider, Restore Physio can treat NSW workers compensation patients without an upfront cost to you — the claim covers the consultation fee.

Our physiotherapists are experienced in workplace injury management and understand the documentation and reporting requirements of the workers compensation system. We work collaboratively with your employer, insurer and treating GP to support your recovery and a safe return to work.
benefits: Approved icare NSW workers compensation provider
No out-of-pocket cost for eligible claims
Experienced in workplace injury documentation and reporting
Return-to-work planning in consultation with your employer
Functional capacity assessment where required
Coordination with treating GP and specialists

sections.heading: How to access treatment under workers compensation
sections.body: If you have been injured at work in NSW, you need to: report the injury to your employer, obtain a WorkCover certificate from your GP (also called a workers compensation medical certificate), and contact your employer's insurer or icare to lodge a claim. Once your claim number is confirmed, contact us and we can schedule your appointment without any upfront payment from you.

sections.heading: Common workplace injuries we treat
sections.body: We regularly treat: back injuries from manual handling and lifting, shoulder injuries from repetitive overhead work, repetitive strain injuries and tendinopathies, neck pain from computer-based work, wrist and hand injuries, knee injuries from kneeling and stair work, and psychological injuries where physiotherapy is part of an approved treatment plan.

faqs.question: Do I need my employer's approval to see a physio for a work injury?
faqs.answer: You do not need your employer's approval to receive initial treatment. Under NSW workers compensation law, you are entitled to access approved treatment. You will need a valid WorkCover medical certificate from your GP and a claim number from the insurer.

faqs.question: What is a functional capacity evaluation and when is it needed?
faqs.answer: A functional capacity evaluation (FCE) is a structured assessment of your physical work capacity. It is sometimes requested by insurers or employers to help plan a return-to-work program. Our physiotherapists are experienced in conducting FCEs and providing written reports.

seo.title: Workers Compensation Physiotherapy | Restore Physio Chatswood
seo.description: Approved icare workers compensation physio in Chatswood, NSW. Workplace injury treatment, functional capacity assessments and return-to-work support for North Shore patients.

services.title: Dry Needling & Manual Therapy
services.summary: Hands-on physiotherapy including dry needling, joint mobilisation and soft-tissue techniques to help manage pain and improve mobility.
services.intro:
Manual therapy and dry needling are hands-on techniques used as part of a broader physiotherapy treatment plan. They are not standalone treatments but can be effective adjuncts to exercise and education when used appropriately.

Our physiotherapists are trained in a range of manual therapy techniques including joint mobilisation, manipulation, soft-tissue therapy, and dry needling. The specific techniques used depend on your assessment findings and your comfort with each approach.
benefits: Dry needling for muscle trigger points and myofascial pain
Joint mobilisation and manipulation for stiff or painful joints
Soft-tissue and myofascial release techniques
Techniques chosen based on your assessment, not a one-size approach
Combined with exercise to support lasting change
Full explanation and consent before any technique is used

sections.heading: What is dry needling?
sections.body: Dry needling involves inserting fine acupuncture-style needles into muscle trigger points. The aim is to reduce local muscle tension and pain sensitivity. It is different from traditional acupuncture in that it is based on anatomical and neurophysiological principles rather than traditional Chinese medicine theory. Some people experience a mild twitch or ache during needling, which is normal.

sections.heading: Conditions where manual therapy may help
sections.body: Manual therapy approaches are used as part of treatment for: neck pain and stiffness, lower back pain, shoulder pain and restricted movement, headaches with a cervical component, hip and knee stiffness, ankle and foot conditions, and thoracic pain. Your physiotherapist will discuss whether these techniques are appropriate for your specific presentation.

faqs.question: Is dry needling the same as acupuncture?
faqs.answer: Dry needling uses similar fine needles to acupuncture but is applied according to musculoskeletal anatomy and neurophysiology rather than traditional acupuncture meridian theory. Physiotherapists who perform dry needling have completed specific training in this technique.

faqs.question: Does dry needling hurt?
faqs.answer: Most people feel minimal discomfort during dry needling. You may feel a brief twitch response in the muscle, which is a normal part of the technique. Some local soreness after the session can occur and usually settles within a day or two.

seo.title: Dry Needling & Manual Therapy | Restore Physio Chatswood
seo.description: Dry needling and manual therapy physiotherapy in Chatswood, North Shore NSW. Registered physiotherapists trained in joint mobilisation, soft-tissue techniques and dry needling.

services.title: Hydrotherapy
services.summary: Supervised aquatic physiotherapy for arthritis, post-surgical recovery, chronic pain and conditions where land-based exercise is limited.
services.intro:
Hydrotherapy uses the properties of warm water to support movement and exercise that may be difficult on land. Buoyancy reduces load on joints, while the resistance and warmth of the water can help with pain management, strength and range of motion.

Our physiotherapist-supervised hydrotherapy programs are conducted at a local heated pool facility near Chatswood. Programs are individually designed and progressed based on your goals.
benefits: Reduced joint load — suitable when land-based exercise is limited
Warm-water environment to support movement and reduce stiffness
Individually designed exercise programs
Suitable for arthritis, post-surgical recovery and chronic conditions
Physiotherapist-supervised sessions
Private health fund rebates available

sections.heading: Who benefits from hydrotherapy?
sections.body: Hydrotherapy is often recommended for patients with osteoarthritis of the hip or knee, rheumatoid arthritis, early post-surgical rehabilitation where land-based loading is restricted, fibromyalgia and chronic pain conditions, neurological conditions affecting balance and mobility, and older adults who find land-based exercise difficult. It is not appropriate for all conditions, and your physiotherapist will assess suitability at your initial consultation.

sections.heading: How are sessions structured?
sections.body: Your first session includes an in-water assessment and introduction to the exercises. Subsequent sessions progress the program based on your tolerance and goals. Sessions are typically 45 minutes. Our physiotherapist attends the pool and supervises exercises directly, monitoring your technique and adjusting the program as needed.

faqs.question: Do I need a referral for hydrotherapy?
faqs.answer: No referral is required for private hydrotherapy. If you are attending under a Medicare chronic disease management plan, a GP referral and management plan are needed. Workers compensation and DVA patients will need relevant approval from their insurer or the Department of Veterans' Affairs.

faqs.question: What should I bring to a hydrotherapy session?
faqs.answer: Bring swimwear, a towel and thongs or sandals for poolside. Water shoes are optional. Lockers and showers are available at the pool facility. If you have any skin conditions or open wounds, please let us know in advance as this may affect suitability.

seo.title: Hydrotherapy | Restore Physio Chatswood North Shore NSW
seo.description: Physiotherapist-supervised hydrotherapy near Chatswood, North Shore. Aquatic physiotherapy for arthritis, post-surgical recovery and chronic pain. Private health fund recognised.

We offer early morning and evening appointments to fit around work and school schedules. Same-day appointments are often available for acute injuries.

benefits: Ground-floor access with disability access available

faqs.question: Is parking available at the Chatswood clinic?
faqs.answer: Street parking is available on Victor Street and nearby streets. Paid parking is also available at Chatswood Chase and the Chatswood station precinct a short walk away.

sections.heading: Treating runners and active residents from Lane Cove
sections.body: Lane Cove has a strong running community, with many residents training along the Lane Cove River Trail and entering events like the City2Surf. Common presentations from active Lane Cove residents include Achilles tendinopathy, plantar fasciitis, IT band syndrome and stress-related injuries. Our physiotherapists can assess your running technique, footwear and training load as part of a comprehensive management plan.

faqs.question: Can you assess my running gait as part of a physio appointment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Running gait analysis can be included as part of your assessment for running-related injuries. Your physiotherapist will observe your gait and may use video analysis to identify contributing movement factors. Please wear your running shoes to the appointment.

service_areas.headline: Sports Injury Rehabilitation in Chatswood
service_areas.intro: Restore Physio in Chatswood provides evidence-based sports injury assessment and rehabilitation for active residents and athletes across the North Shore.
service_areas.body:
Whether you are a competitive athlete or a weekend runner, a sports injury can disrupt your training and daily life. Our registered physiotherapists in Chatswood conduct a thorough assessment of your injury, including movement analysis and load testing, before developing an individualised rehabilitation program.

We treat a wide range of sports injuries in Chatswood including ankle sprains, hamstring strains, shoulder rotator cuff injuries, knee ligament injuries and overuse tendinopathies. Our approach combines manual therapy, targeted exercise and education to help you manage your injury and work toward returning to your activity.
benefits: Evidence-based assessment and treatment from registered physiotherapists
Rehabilitation programs tailored to your sport and return-to-activity goals
Manual therapy and exercise combined for a comprehensive approach
Private health fund recognised — HICAPS claiming on the spot
Same-day and next-day appointments often available in Chatswood

sections.heading: Sports injuries we treat in Chatswood
sections.body: Our Chatswood physiotherapists regularly assess and manage: ankle ligament sprains from court and field sports, hamstring and quadriceps muscle strains, knee ligament and cartilage injuries, shoulder rotator cuff conditions from overhead sports, Achilles tendinopathy in runners, stress fractures in endurance athletes, and wrist and elbow injuries from racquet sports.

faqs.question: How do I get an appointment for sports injury rehab in Chatswood?
faqs.answer: Call us on (02) 9410 5678 or book online. No GP referral is required for private physiotherapy. We offer same-day appointments for acute injuries when available.

service_areas.headline: Back & Neck Pain Physiotherapy for North Sydney
service_areas.intro: Restore Physio in Chatswood provides evidence-based physiotherapy for back and neck pain, serving patients from North Sydney and the CBD corridor.
service_areas.body:
Back and neck pain is one of the most common reasons people seek physiotherapy. For North Sydney professionals spending long hours at a desk, poor workstation setup, prolonged sitting and high work demands can contribute to persistent discomfort. Our physiotherapists assess movement, posture, work habits and lifestyle factors to understand what is driving your pain.

From our Chatswood base, we are accessible to North Sydney patients via a 10-minute train journey on the North Shore line. Early morning and evening appointments are available to fit around CBD work schedules. Treatment may include manual therapy, targeted strengthening, ergonomic advice and pain education.
benefits: Detailed assessment covering posture, movement and work demands
Practical ergonomic advice for desk-based workers
Manual therapy and targeted exercise combined
Easy train access from North Sydney for CBD workers
Early morning and evening appointments available

sections.heading: Desk workers and postural pain
sections.body: North Sydney has a large professional workforce, and desk-related back and neck pain is extremely common. We regularly provide workstation ergonomics advice alongside hands-on physiotherapy to address the physical factors contributing to pain. Simple changes to screen height, chair setup and movement habits can make a meaningful difference when combined with targeted treatment.

faqs.question: Can you help with neck pain from sitting at a computer all day?
faqs.answer: Yes. Cervicogenic (neck-related) pain from prolonged desk work is one of the most common presentations we see from North Sydney patients. Treatment typically includes manual therapy, postural correction exercises and ergonomic guidance.

Common injuries we see from this cohort include running-related lower limb injuries, gym-related shoulder conditions, and contact sport knee and ankle injuries. Our physiotherapists understand the demands of these activities and incorporate sport-specific work into rehabilitation programs.

service_areas.headline: Post-Surgical Rehabilitation for Willoughby Patients
service_areas.intro: Restore Physio in Chatswood provides structured post-surgical physiotherapy for patients from Willoughby recovering from joint replacement, ligament surgery and other procedures.
service_areas.body:
Post-surgical rehabilitation helps you regain mobility, strength and function after an operation. For Willoughby residents, our Chatswood clinic is minutes away via the Pacific Highway or Mowbray Road, making it a convenient option for the regular appointments that post-surgical rehab typically requires.

Our physiotherapists work from protocols aligned with your surgeon's post-operative instructions. We progress your program carefully, monitoring for signs of swelling, pain or limited movement that may require liaison with your surgical team. Milestones and progress are documented throughout.
benefits: Protocols aligned with your surgeon's specific post-operative guidelines
Short drive from Willoughby via Pacific Highway
Experienced in knee, hip, shoulder and spinal post-surgical rehab
Regular documentation and progress reporting
Private health fund recognised for post-surgical consultations

sections.heading: Common post-surgical pathways for Willoughby patients
sections.body: Willoughby residents commonly access us following procedures at Mater Hospital, Royal North Shore Hospital and local private hospitals. Frequent post-surgical presentations include total knee replacement, total hip replacement, ACL reconstruction, rotator cuff repair and spinal procedures. We are experienced in these pathways and can liaise with your surgical team directly.

faqs.question: How soon after surgery should I start physiotherapy?
faqs.answer: This depends on the type of surgery. Some procedures benefit from physiotherapy within the first few days; others require a period of rest or protected movement. Your surgeon will advise, and we can coordinate with their rooms to clarify the recommended start time and any restrictions.

sections.heading: Workers compensation claims from Artarmon
sections.body: Eligible workers from Artarmon businesses can access physiotherapy at Restore Physio without paying out of pocket. You will need a valid WorkCover certificate from your GP and a claim number from your employer's insurer or icare. Once these are confirmed, contact us to schedule your appointments. We handle all billing directly with the insurer.

faqs.question: I was injured at work in Artarmon. What are my next steps?
faqs.answer: Report your injury to your employer, see your GP to obtain a WorkCover certificate, and contact your employer's insurer or icare to lodge a claim. Once you have a claim number, contact us and we can book your physiotherapy appointment without any out-of-pocket expense.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Common questions about physiotherapy at Restore Physio, fees, rebates and what to expect.

items.question: Do I need a referral from my GP to see a physiotherapist?
items.answer: No. You can book directly with a physiotherapist without a GP referral for private consultations. A GP referral is required if you wish to use a Medicare chronic disease management plan (which subsidises a limited number of sessions) or if you are claiming through workers compensation or DVA.

items.question: Does private health insurance cover physiotherapy?
items.answer: Most private health insurance extras policies include physiotherapy. Rebates vary by fund and policy level. We have HICAPS on site so you can claim on the spot and pay only the gap. We recommend calling your fund to confirm your physiotherapy benefit before your appointment.

items.question: What should I bring to my first appointment?
items.answer: Please bring your private health insurance card if applicable, any relevant imaging (X-rays, MRI, ultrasound), a referral letter or WorkCover certificate if you have one, and any relevant medical history paperwork. Wear comfortable clothing that allows access to the area being assessed.

items.question: How long is a physiotherapy appointment?
items.answer: Initial appointments are 60 minutes and include a detailed assessment and the start of treatment. Follow-up appointments are typically 30 to 45 minutes. Specific appointment lengths vary by treatment type — hydrotherapy sessions are 45 minutes and workplace injury initial assessments may be up to 60 minutes.

items.question: Are you registered with AHPRA?
items.answer: Yes. All physiotherapists at Restore Physio hold current registration with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (AHPRA). You can verify any physiotherapist's registration at the AHPRA website.

items.question: Do you offer Medicare-subsidised physiotherapy?
items.answer: Physiotherapy is not covered under standard Medicare. However, patients with a chronic condition and an active Enhanced Primary Care (EPC) or Chronic Disease Management (CDM) plan from their GP may be eligible for a Medicare rebate on up to 5 allied health services per calendar year. Ask your GP if you may be eligible.

items.question: Can I see a physio for NDIS-funded treatment?
items.answer: We see NDIS participants who are plan-managed or self-managed. Agency-managed participants may need to confirm that Restore Physio is a registered NDIS provider before booking. Please contact us to discuss your NDIS plan and funding details.

items.question: What is the cancellation policy?
items.answer: We ask for at least 24 hours notice to cancel or reschedule an appointment. Cancellations with less than 24 hours notice may incur a cancellation fee. This policy helps us offer appointment times to other patients who need them.

seo.title: Physiotherapy FAQ | Restore Physio Chatswood North Shore
seo.description: Answers to common questions about physiotherapy at Restore Physio in Chatswood. Rebates, referrals, what to bring, Medicare, private health and workers compensation explained.

about.heading: About Restore Physio
about.body:
Restore Physio has been providing evidence-based physiotherapy to patients on Sydney's North Shore for 9 years. Our clinic is based in Chatswood, with a team of registered physiotherapists who hold current AHPRA registration and maintain their professional development through ongoing education and peer supervision.

We treat the full range of musculoskeletal and sports conditions, from acute injuries to chronic pain presentations. We also work with post-surgical patients, workplace injury claimants, DVA patients and people with chronic conditions accessing treatment through Medicare enhanced primary care plans.

Our approach is straightforward: we take the time to properly assess what is driving your problem, explain it to you clearly, and develop a treatment plan that makes sense for your life and goals. We do not recommend more treatment than is necessary.

values.title: Evidence-based practice
values.body: Our treatment decisions are guided by current clinical evidence and best-practice guidelines. We do not recommend treatments that lack a sound evidence base.

values.title: Patient-centred care
values.body: We involve you in all decisions about your treatment. You will always know what we found in your assessment, what we recommend and why, and what to expect from the treatment plan.

values.title: Transparent fees
values.body: Our fees are published and discussed upfront. There are no surprise charges. We provide HICAPS claiming so you pay only the gap after your health fund rebate.

seo.title: About Restore Physio | Registered Physiotherapists Chatswood
seo.description: About Restore Physio in Chatswood, NSW. AHPRA-registered physiotherapists with 9 years of practice on Sydney's North Shore. Evidence-based treatment, transparent fees.
